title: fed / balance sheet / quantitative tightening | Balance Sheet Update | Plans for Reducing the Size of the Federal Reserve's Balance...
summary: The Federal Reserve announced plans for quantitative tightening beginning June 1, 2022, with monthly caps on securities runoff set at $30 billion for Treasuries and $17.5 billion for agency MBS initially, doubling to $60 billion and $35 billion respectively after three months. All FOMC participants agreed to the plan, which is designed to reduce the balance sheet in a predictable manner while maintaining an ample reserves regime.
